LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF HENRY WOLF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AUGMENT EXPERT DISCLOSURES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING TO WARRANT LEAVE TO AUGMENT EXPERT DISCLOSURE. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MADE AN ADEQUATE SHOWING THAT THE ORIGINAL DISCLOSURE WAS THE RESULT OF "MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT." (CCP 2034.620(B).) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CONTEND THAT DR. MCINTOSH OR DR. RUTNICH IS UNAVAILABLE, NOR DOES HE CONTEND THAT THEY HAVE FLIPPED ON PREVIOUSLY EXPRESSED OPINIONS. RATHER, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS SIMPLY DECIDED, AFTER THE FACT, THAT HE WOULD HAVE TAKEN A DIFFERENT APPROACH TO EXPERTS THAN THE APPROACH TAKEN BY FORMER COUNSEL. AS PLAINTIFF'S CURRENT COUNSEL STATES IN HIS DECLARATION, HE "WOULD HAVE DONE THINGS MUCH, MUCH DIFFERENTLY" THAN FORMER COUNSEL. (MARTIN DECL., 7.) THIS DOES NOT MAKE THE ORIGINAL DISCLOSURE THE RESULT OF "MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT." (CCP 2034.620(B).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NORA E. WETZEL OF SEDGWICK LLP, TEL. NO. 781-7900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA LLC;
ATTY. TEELA A. CROSTHWAITE, TEL. NO. (650) 989-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CORBIN-PACIFIC.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF HENRY WOLF's Motion For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures is Denied. Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to warrant leave to augment expert disclosure. Plaintiff has not made an adequate showing that the original disclosure was the result of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP 2034.620(b).) Plaintiff does not contend that Dr. McIntosh or Dr. Rutnich is unavailable, nor does he contend that they have flipped on previously expressed opinions. Rather, it appears that Plaintiff has simply decided, after the fact, that he would have taken a different approach to experts than the approach taken by former counsel. As Plaintiff's current counsel states in his declaration, he "would have done things much, much differently" than former counsel. (Martin Decl., 7.) This does not make the original disclosure the result of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP 2034.620(b).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/AR
